title: chabraea glandulosa
intro: Chabraea glandulosa, also known as the Indian snakeroot, is a plant species that belongs to the family Apocynaceae and is native to the tropical regions of Asia. The medicinal properties of Chabraea glandulosa include its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antipyretic properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Chabraea glandulosa include its ability to relieve muscle spasms, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Chabraea glandulosa can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal uses.
